projects.title: Face Rekognition
projects.description: IoT-powered automated attendance system using ESP32-CAM, AWS IoT Core, Lambda, and AWS Rekognition. Serverless architecture with face detection and real-time processing.
projects.extended_description: Built as an academic production-grade prototype, this project focused on low-latency recognition events, robust event ingestion, and auditable attendance records across unreliable network conditions.
projects.problem: Manual attendance workflows are error-prone and slow, especially in classrooms where time-to-start matters.
projects.approach: Combined edge camera capture with a serverless event pipeline. Recognition events flow through AWS IoT and Lambda, then persist to DynamoDB with timestamped logs for traceability.
projects.outcome: Delivered a working end-to-end attendance flow with near real-time updates, reproducible infra behavior, and clear monitoring points for failure analysis.
highlights: ESP32-CAM powered capture at the edge
Event-driven backend with AWS IoT Core and Lambda
Serverless architecture optimized for low ops overhead

projects.title: Beat.pe
projects.description: A web-based rhythm game where timing is everything. Built entirely from scratch with vanilla JavaScript and CSS animations.
projects.extended_description: This was a deliberate exercise in browser performance, deterministic timing, and responsive game UX without framework abstractions.
projects.problem: Most rhythm game prototypes feel laggy on low-end laptops because input handling and animation updates drift over time.
projects.approach: Implemented frame-aware timing windows, stateful input handling, and lightweight animation orchestration using vanilla JavaScript and CSS.
projects.outcome: Shipped a smooth browser game with stable interactions, responsive layout behavior, and fast initial load for casual play sessions.
highlights: Responsive layout tuned for laptop and mobile

projects.title: Listly
projects.description: Terminal-based music playlist manager built from scratch using custom doubly linked lists. Features persistent file storage and an ANSI-colored CLI.
projects.extended_description: Listly was designed as a data-structure-first project to practice writing maintainable core logic before polishing interface ergonomics.
highlights: Persistent storage via plain file I/O
Clear separation between model and command actions

projects.title: dhanur.me
projects.description: The site you're looking at right now. Fast, static, and occasionally broken when I push to main at 3 AM.
projects.extended_description: This portfolio is built to stay lightweight while still supporting search, taxonomy navigation, performance-first rendering, and maintainable content generation workflows.
highlights: Zola static architecture with data-driven content generation
Unified taxonomy system across blog, about, and projects
Tailwind + DaisyUI component styling with custom polish
Automated project page sync integrated into build pipeline

projects.title: stuff

projects.extended_description: This is my practical junk drawer: tiny CLIs, helper scripts, and quality-of-life automations I keep building whenever something feels repetitive.
highlights: Personal tools focused on daily workflow friction
Small utilities designed for fast iteration
Actively evolving with new scripts over time
